---
title: "Day 5: Florence - Bargello & Santa Croce"
subtitle: "Sculpture & Florentine Tombs"
date: "2026-06-09"
day-of-week: "Monday"
location: "Florence"
categories: [itinerary, florence, museums]
---

## Overview

**Focus:** Bargello Museum (sculpture), Santa Croce church, local neighborhoods

---

## Morning: Bargello Museum (9-11 AM)

**World's Best Renaissance Sculpture Collection**

**Highlights:**
- Donatello's bronze *David* (first nude since antiquity!)
- Michelangelo early works
- Verrocchio sculptures
- Della Robbia ceramics

**Why It's Great:**
- Often overlooked - fewer crowds than Uffizi
- Incredible collection in medieval palace
- 1.5-2 hours sufficient

---

## Late Morning: Santa Croce (11:30 AM-12:30 PM)

**Franciscan Church - Pantheon of Florence**

**Tombs of:**
- Michelangelo
- Galileo
- Machiavelli
- Rossini

**Also:**
- Giotto frescoes (Bardi and Peruzzi Chapels)
- Pazzi Chapel (Brunelleschi)
- Leather school (free entry from side)

**Time:** 45-60 minutes

---

## Afternoon: Santa Croce Neighborhood (1-5 PM)

**Lunch:** Trattoria near Santa Croce piazza

**Explore:**
- Piazza Santa Croce (lively square)
- Via de' Benci (restaurants, bars)
- Local shops and markets
- Less touristy than Duomo area

**Activities:**
- Shopping for souvenirs
- Leather goods
- Paper products (Florence famous for)
- Gelato tasting (#3 this trip?)

---

## Evening: Casual Night

**Dinner:** Local trattoria, relaxed

**After:** Evening stroll, pack for tomorrow's departure to Naples

---

**Previous:** [Day 4](day-04-2026-06-08-florence.qmd) | **Next:** [Day 6](day-06-2026-06-10-florence.qmd)
